explicit_text: I've been the CEO of Marias Healthcare for over 18 years. We started as a private practice and grew into a community health center offering comprehensive, patient-centered care. During the COVID-19 pandemic, I had to shift from hands-on operations to managing regional coordination and constantly updating staff on evolving regulations. Marias Healthcare played a critical role in keeping our doors open when many hospitals closed, helping to manage community health and reduce pressure on local hospitals.

During the COVID-19 pandemic, I had to shift from hands-on operations to managing regional coordination and constantly updating staff on evolving regulations. This organization played a critical role in keeping our doors open when many hospitals closed, helping to manage community health and reduce pressure on local hospitals.

explicit_text: Personally, I am a farm and ranch girl who loves spending time in the countryside and the Rocky Mountain front. The Rocky Mountains are a place I disappear to for peace and connection with nature. They are part of my roots and lifestyle outside of my professional work in healthcare.
implicit_text: Personally, I am a farm and ranch girl who loves spending time in the countryside and the mountain front that borders our region. Those mountains are a place I disappear to for peace and connection with nature. They are part of my roots and lifestyle outside of my professional work in healthcare.

explicit_text: The COVID-19 pandemic drastically changed my role as CEO of Marias Healthcare. I went from being very hands-on to focusing on managing information flow, regional coordination, and resource sharing among local health entities. Shelby, Montana became a hotspot early on, and we faced challenges like PPE shortages and outbreaks at the Heritage Center. We had to adapt quickly with new protocols, home visits, telehealth, and community support to keep our patients safe and care accessible.
implicit_text: The global viral outbreak drastically changed my role as CEO of the community health organization. I went from being very hands-on to focusing on managing information flow, regional coordination, and resource sharing among local health entities. Shelby, Montana became a hotspot early on, and we faced challenges like PPE shortages and outbreaks at the local assisted living facility. We had to adapt quickly with new protocols, home visits, telehealth, and community support to keep our patients safe and care accessible.

explicit_text: During the pandemic, I worked closely with leaders from Indian Health Service as part of a regional incident command. We collaborated with various health organizations and public officials to share resources and coordinate responses. Indian Health Service was one of the key partners in managing the crisis in our area.
implicit_text: During the pandemic, I worked closely with leaders from the federal agency responsible for Native American health as part of a regional incident command. We collaborated with various health organizations and public officials to share resources and coordinate responses. That federal agency was one of the key partners in managing the crisis in our area.

explicit_text: Shelby, Montana was at the center of the COVID-19 outbreak in our region and became a hotspot early in the pandemic. As the CEO of Marias Healthcare, I saw firsthand how the clinic and hospital responded differently to the crisis. We kept our clinic doors open to serve the community while the hospital adopted a closed-door policy. Shelby's community rallied together, sewing masks and gowns, and we coordinated with local facilities like the Heritage Center to manage outbreaks and care for vulnerable populations.
implicit_text: Shelby, Montana was at the center of the viral outbreak in our region and became a hotspot early in the pandemic. We kept our clinic doors open to serve the community while the hospital adopted a closed-door policy. Shelby's community rallied together, sewing masks and gowns, and we coordinated with local facilities like the assisted living center to manage outbreaks and care for vulnerable populations.

explicit_text: Montana is where I live and work, specifically in rural and frontier areas like Shelby and Toole County. The state faced unique challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic, including limited healthcare workforce and PPE shortages. We had to innovate and collaborate across communities to manage the crisis, including drawing inspiration from other states like Maine for community support initiatives.

The state faced unique challenges during the pandemic, including limited healthcare workforce and PPE shortages. We had to innovate and collaborate across communities to manage the crisis, including drawing inspiration from other states like the one in New England for community support initiatives.

explicit_text: Seattle was an early indicator of the COVID-19 pandemic’s impact, and I stayed connected with healthcare providers there to learn from their experiences. My daughter and some family members of colleagues work in Seattle hospitals, which helped us receive timely information to prepare our protocols in Montana. This connection was invaluable as we monitored the virus’s spread and planned our response.
implicit_text: A major city in the Pacific Northwest was an early indicator of the pandemic’s impact, and I stayed connected with healthcare providers there to learn from their experiences. My daughter and some family members of colleagues work in hospitals in that city, which helped us receive timely information to prepare our protocols in Montana. This connection was invaluable as we monitored the virus’s spread and planned our response.

explicit_text: We maintained connections with the University of Washington during the pandemic to stay informed on best practices and emerging data. This relationship, along with ties to Seattle hospitals, helped guide our COVID-19 response in Montana. The UW was part of the network that supported our regional healthcare coordination efforts.
implicit_text: We maintained connections with a prominent public university in the Pacific Northwest during the pandemic to stay informed on best practices and emerging data. This relationship, along with ties to hospitals in the nearby major city, helped guide our COVID-19 response in Montana. That university was part of the network that supported our regional healthcare coordination efforts.

explicit_text: Billings was one of the cities in Montana where COVID-19 cases appeared before reaching our area. We had protocols ready for when the virus hit Billings and then Shelby, but the virus leapfrogged directly to Shelby, challenging our preparedness. We coordinated with regional hospitals including those in Billings to manage patient flow and resources during the pandemic.
implicit_text: A city in southern Montana was one of the places where COVID-19 cases appeared before reaching our area. We had protocols ready for when the virus hit that city and then Shelby, but the virus leapfrogged directly to Shelby, challenging our preparedness. We coordinated with regional hospitals including those in that southern city to manage patient flow and resources during the pandemic.

explicit_text: Great Falls was another regional hub affected by COVID-19, and we included it in our planning as the virus spread across Montana. We coordinated with hospitals in Great Falls to share patients and resources, aiming to keep beds and workforce available across the region. This collaboration was crucial for managing the pandemic in rural Montana.

We coordinated with hospitals there to share patients and resources, aiming to keep beds and workforce available across the region. This collaboration was crucial for managing the pandemic in rural Montana.

explicit_text: The Heritage Center in Shelby is a retirement and assisted living facility with about 32 apartments, housing many of our elderly community members. During the COVID-19 outbreak, it became a major exposure site, especially because of family-style dining and social activities. We worked closely with the Heritage Center staff to isolate residents, manage care onsite, and support families through difficult decisions about treatment and end-of-life care.

We worked closely with the facility staff to isolate residents, manage care onsite, and support families through difficult decisions about treatment and end-of-life care.

explicit_text: Interestingly, some of the gowns and masks sewn by community members in Montana were also sent to New York City, which was another early COVID-19 hotspot. This connection showed how small communities like ours contributed to the broader national response. It was inspiring to see local efforts support places as far away as New York during the crisis.
implicit_text: Interestingly, some of the gowns and masks sewn by community members in Montana were also sent to a major East Coast city, which was another early COVID-19 hotspot. This connection showed how small communities like ours contributed to the broader national response. It was inspiring to see local efforts support places as far away as that city during the crisis.

explicit_text: Toole County is the rural area where Shelby and Marias Healthcare are located. Serving this frontier county means facing challenges like limited healthcare workforce and resources. During the pandemic, we had to be especially vigilant and creative to protect our community and maintain access to care in Toole County.

Serving this county means facing challenges like limited healthcare workforce and resources. During the pandemic, we had to be especially vigilant and creative to protect our community and maintain access to care in this rural county.

explicit_text: We borrowed a community support idea from Maine during the pandemic, where behavioral health teams painted yellow hearts around town to uplift spirits. This 'small town, big hearts' initiative was done quietly overnight and brought a sense of hope and connection to our Montana community. It was a beautiful example of how ideas can travel and inspire across states.
